rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DENIS JOSEPH MURPHY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Proposed Action; Objection To Administrator?S Status Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/31/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been submitted. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] (3) No proof of service of notice has been filed. (Notice must also include the procedures for making a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.]) (4) The administrator's report of status of administration was already approved on 9/18/23, so any objection to that report of status is too late. (A new status hearing has been set for 3/18/24 for tracking the close of the estate.) Please provide the hard copy proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 1/22/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MARY LHERON MEANS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/3/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) If an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (2) The proposed distribution appears to be incorrect if the executor has waived her statutory compensation. (In any event, cash is cash, regardless of the account out of which it is disbursed/distributed, so the proposed distribution should simply show total cash, less fees and costs.) (3) This Court does not ordinarily authorize payment of "anticipated" costs. Costs actually incurred will be allowed; a reserve is ordinarily requested. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/20/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CATHI ANN COCHRANE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/29/2024, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (see tentative ruling for past hearings - also e-mailed to petitioner in pro per on 10/10/2023): 1) No proposed order (DE-226 form) has been received or lodged. 2) No DE-120 form is on file. A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) Item 4c of the petition: At least one box must be checked. 4) Item 7: i) Attachment 7 must be filed to allege facts in support of property characterization. For community property, allegations required by LRSF 14.26(A) must be included. ii) Attachment 7a must be filed. The property determined to be passing to the petitioner must be described. Property characterization (decedent's separate property, her ½ share of community property, or both) must also be alleged. 5) Item 9: i) Please provide the names and dates of death of the decedent's parents. ii) Is Candy Hirsch the only sibling the decedent ever had? 6) If the vehicle is the only property subject to this petition, please explain why you are not proceeding with the affidavit procedure under Vehicle Code section 5910 (affidavit for transfer without probate). An appearance may be required on 01/29/2024 if no satisfactory progress is shown to address the issues.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BELEN PATRICIA BARRERA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/8/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must include the procedures for making a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) Item 1 of the Notice did not identify the decedent. (3) No proof of publication of the notice has been filed yet. (4) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (5) Items 5a(3) or (4) and 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered in a supplement, prepared on pleading paper and signed and verified by the petitioner. (6) Item 8 must list all heirs of the decedent. Who is/was the decedent's father? He must receive or waive notice. If he is deceased, his date of death must be set forth. If he post-deceased, his successor-in-interest must be identified and must receive notice. (7) If the father is living (or his successor(s)-in-interest if he post-deceased), he must also waive bond if the Court is to appoint without bond. (Who is Hilda Barrera?) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 12/22/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE THOMAS RICHARD JUHL 2008 REVOCABLE TRUST, AS AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Instructions Concerning Trust Amendment
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 01/08/2024. 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) Eileen Donahue and Brian Godfrey must sign their response. CRC 7.103(a). The response must also be verified. CRC 7.103(b) & (c). 3) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 30 days before the hearing. 4) The proof of service attached to the DE-120 form on file did not list University of California San Francisco Foundation. 5) Please provide the date of death of Thomas Christian Juhl.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SEGUNDO AND LILY FORONDA TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Modification Of Segundo And Lily Foronda Trust
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing (NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] A petitioner is required to provide a hard copy proposed order and courtesy copies. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E. and 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.])

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ADRIAN CARPENTIER
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.
